remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Dataiku named a Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for 5th consecutive year
remarks.content: For the fifth consecutive year, Dataiku has been named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for AI Platforms for Data Science and Machine Learning, once again recognized in the Leader quadrant for both “Completeness of Vision” and “Ability to Execute.”
remarks.contentTranslation: Dataiku连续第五年被评为2026年Gartner ®数据科学和机器学习AI平台魔力象限™的领导者，再次在“愿景的完整性”和“执行能力”的领导者象限中获得认可。
remarks.translation: Dataiku连续第五年被评为Gartner魔力象限领导者
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Lilian Weng's Blog (OpenAI)
remarks.role: AI research deep-dives, LLM architectures, agent systems, multimodal, alignment, safety.
remarks.text: Scaling Laws, Carefully
remarks.content: Scaling laws are one of the most critical empirical findings in deep learning. The observation is simple in form: the training loss $L$ decreases predictably as we scale up model size $N$, dataset size $D$, and compute $C$, following a power-law curve, which appears as a straight line on a log-log p
remarks.contentTranslation: 缩放定律是深度学习中最关键的经验发现之一。观察结果的形式很简单：当我们按照幂律曲线（在对数对数p上显示为直线）按比例放大模型大小$ N $、数据集大小$ D $和计算$ C $时，训练损失$ L $可预测地减少
remarks.translation: 谨慎地扩展法律
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: AI explainability in finance: auditable models, GenAI, and agents
remarks.content: Regulators are asking financial institutions a direct question: Can you explain how your AI made that decision? For most, the honest answer is no — not fully, not on demand, and not across every system that now influences regulated decisions. Compliance functions are expected to cover
remarks.contentTranslation: 监管机构正在向金融机构提出一个直接的问题：你能解释一下你的AI是如何做出这个决定的吗？对于大多数人来说，诚实的答案是否定的—不是完全的，不是按需提供的，也不是每个现在影响受监管决策的系统。合规职能预计将涵盖
remarks.translation: 财务中的AI可解释性：可审计模型、GenAI和Agent商
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: The AI success gap: why more AI doesn’t add up to more value
remarks.content: If AI is everywhere, why are so few companies getting real value from it?That is the question most enterprises are now facing. Eighty-eight percent of companies use AI, but only six percent generate meaningful value from it, according to a study from McKinsey.Pilots run, demos impress, a
remarks.contentTranslation: 如果AI无处不在，为什么很少有公司从中获得真正的价值？这是大多数企业现在面临的问题。根据麦肯锡的一项研究，百分之八十八的公司使用AI，但只有百分之六的公司从中产生有意义的价值。
remarks.translation: AI成功差距：为什么更多的AI不能带来更多的价值
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
Tons of improvements landed in Codex. 

- Handles super long threads smoothly. 
- Hoverable navigation rail for previewing and jumping between turns that feels just right. 
- Settings search covers more controls, with clearer appearance and host-filtering options and easier-to-find custom-provider settings. 
- Zoom-level changes no longer misalign tooltips, dialogs, menus, selection bubbles, drag previews, or autocomplete. 
- Copying into Slack preserves Markdown formatting such as bullets, bold text, code, and links; and large text pastes no longer freeze the UI.
- And most importantly: a dedicated Pets panel.
remarks.translation:
Codex进行了大量改进。-处理超长螺纹顺畅。-悬停导航栏，用于预览和转弯之间的跳跃，感觉恰到好处。-设置搜索涵盖更多控件，具有更清晰的外观和主机筛选选项，以及更易于查找的自定义提供商设置。-缩放级别的更改不再未对齐工具提示、对话框、菜单、选择气泡、拖动预览或自动完成。
-复制到Slack会保留Markdown格式，如项目符号、粗体文本、代码和链接；大文本粘贴不再冻结用户界面。-最重要的是：专门的宠物面板。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
There's just one small problem with this framework:

If you wait till level 5 or whatever to tell other people that there's a problem when there's been a burning fire for days and your "solution" actually didn't fix the problem at all, you're 100% in a worse situation than someone who is level 1 and just told other people about the problem right away and found a solution together.

There's no black and white to this stuff, everything has nuance.
remarks.translation: 这个框架只有一个小问题：如果你等到5级或其他什么级别才告诉其他人，当火灾已经燃烧了好几天，而你的“解决方案”实际上根本没有解决问题时，你的情况比1级人更糟糕，只是马上告诉其他人这个问题，并一起找到了解决方案。这东西没有黑白之分，一切都有细微差别。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
Mythos / Sol cybersecurity capabilities are equally useful in an offensive as well a defensive capacity.

If adversaries get ahold of an equivalent offensive capability, it poses a serious threat to US companies that remain unaware of latent vulnerabilities.

In the meantime, I strongly recommend running deepsec[1] or similar harnesses with the available frontier models.

[1] https://t.co/Wh4QVGDFnm
remarks.translation: Mythos/Sol网络安全能力在进攻和防御能力方面同样有用。如果对手掌握了同等的进攻能力，就会对仍未意识到潜在漏洞的美国公司构成严重威胁。与此同时，我强烈建议使用可用的前沿模型运行deepsec [1]或类似的线束。[1]
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Matt Turck
remarks.role: VC at FirstMarkCap.  Host: MAD Podcast; Organizer: Data Driven NYC, Author: MAD Landscape.
remarks.text:
Smart glasses and goggles, a history:

Silicon Valley, 2013 (Google): “you really want this”
Everyone: “no we don’t”

Silicon Valley, 2016 (Microsoft): “ok but what if it’s for the enterprise”
Enterprise: “maybe, but also, no”

Silicon Valley, 2023 (Meta): “ok but what if they look normal and have AI”
Everyone: “wait… maybe? … Actually, no”

Silicon Valley, 2024 (Apple): “ok but what if it’s $3,499 and covers your whole face”
Everyone: “absolutely not”

Silicon Valley, 2026 (Snap): “ok but this time for real”
Everyone: “we admire the persistence but still no”
remarks.translation:
智能眼镜和护目镜，历史：硅谷， 2013年（谷歌） ： “你真的想要这个”每个人： “不，我们不想”硅谷， 2016年（微软） ： “好吧，但如果它是为了企业”企业： “也许，但也，不”硅谷， 2023年（元） ： “好吧，但如果他们看起来正常并且拥有AI”每个人： “等等......也许？
……实际上，没有”硅谷， 2024年（苹果） ：“好吧，但如果它是$ 3,499并覆盖你的整个脸怎么办”每个人：“绝对没有”硅谷， 2026年（快照） ：“好吧，但这次是真的”每个人：“我们钦佩这种坚持，但仍然没有”
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
An interesting way to take Noam at his word in regards to always keeping a constant inference budget for any eval reporting -

is that open models have a lot more dollar per token mileage than closed model APIs. So anyone launching an open model today or situationally incentivized toward open models should obviously report thinking levels measured by dollar inference on popular inference providers, instead of by number of tokens on the x axis
remarks.translation: 在为任何评估报告始终保持恒定的推理预算方面，让Noam信守诺言的一个有趣方法是，开放模型的每个代币里程比封闭模型API要多得多。因此，任何今天推出开放模型或受到情境激励而转向开放模型的人显然都应该报告通过流行推理提供商的美元推理来衡量的思维水平，而不是x轴上的代币数量
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
Some good best practices here on AI token cost optimization. None of these happens though without a deep understanding of the underlying work being done in a non-abstract way.

The ultimate implication is that a layer between the work itself and the underlying intelligence needs to deeply understand your workflows, context, and business process. Now, each individual company doing this on their own is unlikely to be effective at scale, so as a consequence, this is effectively the playbook for any applied AI company right now. 

By evaling the models for the applied use cases, deeply understanding the domain, having tuned UX and features for the use case, and having the ability to support adoption and change (via FDEs), allow this layer to add a ton of value. And as a result, enterprises get higher ROI because you actually can get *more* intelligence per dollar by having optimal architecture and workflows.

There will be many horizontal and vertical versions of this approach. Huge opportunity right now.
remarks.translation:
这里有一些关于AI代币成本优化的最佳实践。但是，如果没有深入了解以非抽象方式完成的基本工作，这些都不会发生。最终的含义是，工作本身和底层智能之间的一层需要深入了解您的工作流、上下文和业务流程。
现在，每个单独的公司单独这样做不太可能在规模上有效，因此，这实际上是目前任何应用AI公司的剧本。通过评估应用用例的模型，深入了解领域，针对用例调整用户体验和功能，并有能力（通过FDE ）支持采用和变革，使这一层能够增加大量价值。
因此，企业获得了更高的投资回报率，因为通过优化的架构和工作流，您实际上可以获得每美元*更多*的智能。这种方法将有许多水平和垂直版本。现在是巨大的机会。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

As someone who eats ice cream ~daily, this was SO fun to read.. 

Also, the visuals are absolutely stunning!! https://t.co/4ZelLvM5XS

remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
Every Saturday, Hermes sends me a health check email with top takeaways and stats to help me work toward my health goals by pulling data from:

→ My smart scale via the Withings API
→ My Fitbit and Google Health
→ An MCP server and mobile fitness app I vibe coded to track my workouts

I find this super useful, even if my body fat is going the wrong way 😅

I talk about how to do this in my Hermes tutorial here: https://t.co/p0BDhL6PAp

Should I make another video on building my fitness app?
remarks.translation: 每周六，爱马仕都会向我发送一封健康检查电子邮件，其中包含热门要点和统计数据，以帮助我通过以下方式→实现健康目标：通过Withings API → My Fitbit和Google Health获取我的智→能体重秤MCP服务器和移动健身应用程序我用VIBE编码来跟踪我的锻炼，我觉得这非常有用，即使我的身体脂肪状况不佳，😅我也会在爱马仕教程中谈论如何做到这一点：我是否应该制作另一个关于构建健身应用程序的视频？
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。
